definition: Technologies that enable a vehicle to perceive its environment and drive itself with reduced or no human control — i.e. autonomous / self-driving vehicles and the perception, localization, planning, and control that make automated driving possible.

in_scope_tasks.desc: autonomous / self-driving vehicle control (the vehicle drives itself; SAE L3-L5 automated driving)
environment perception for driving: object/lane/traffic-sign detection, sensor fusion (LiDAR/radar/camera) for automated driving
motion planning, trajectory generation, path/decision planning for an automated vehicle
localization / HD-mapping / SLAM used for automated driving
V2X / connected-vehicle communication that supports automated driving
teleoperation or remote driving of an autonomous vehicle

out_of_scope_confusables: ADAS / driver-assistance that keeps the human driver in control (lane-keep warning, blind-spot alert, adaptive cruise as assist)
general vehicle/automotive engineering not about autonomy (powertrain, steering hardware, suspension, body)
generic computer vision / object detection not applied to driving
generic robotics / unmanned ground vehicles not road vehicles
in-cabin infotainment, navigation maps for human drivers, insurance/telematics
traffic infrastructure or driver-monitoring without vehicle autonomy
hard_negative_patterns: driver-assistance feature that only warns or assists while the human keeps driving
obstacle/collision warning for a human-driven vehicle
vehicle component (brake, steering, motor) with no autonomous-driving decision making
object recognition described generically with a vehicle only as an example
insurance / data-logging / telematics mentioning autonomous vehicles but not implementing driving

score_anchors.0.9_1.0: explicitly performs an in_scope_task with a concrete mechanism (the vehicle drives/plans/perceives autonomously)
score_anchors.0.6_0.75: automated-driving task implied but mechanism unclear, or mixed assist/autonomy signals -> boundary
score_anchors.0.0_0.25: no autonomous-driving signal, or only a confusable signal (driver-assist / generic vehicle / generic CV)
